paper_title: Construction of In2S3@NH2\u2011MIL-68(In)@In2S3 Sandwich Homologous Heterojunction for Efficient CO2 Photoreduction

catalyst.source: Herein, we construct an In 2 S 3 NH 2 -MIL-68(In) In 2 S 3 (denoted as SMS(In)) sandwich homologous heterojunction via a controllable in situ metal-organic frameworks (MOF) sulfuration process.
catalyst.context: Photocatalytic conversion of CO 2 into fuel and value-added chemicals as a promising method has been applied recently to ease energy-or environment-related crises.Herein, we construct an In 2 S 3 NH 2 -MIL-68(In) In 2 S 3 (denoted as SMS(In)) sandwich homologous heterojunction via a controllable in situ metal-organic frameworks (MOF) sulfuration process.The improved photoreduction activity mainly lies in the effective charge transfer and separation through a homogeneous interface, enhanced CO 2 adsorption and mass transfer, and plentiful reactive sites.Benefiting from this unique hollow homologous heterojunction, SMS(In)-7 obtains a CO generation rate as high as 11.23 mol g -1 h -1 with visible-light irradiation, nearly 3 times enhancement over that of pure MOF-derived In 2 S 3 .This work provides a novel insight into the utilization of MOF-based platform for an efficient atomically heterojunction design.

catalyst.source: As a consequence, the optimum sample (SMS(In)-7) exhibited outstanding photocatalytic activity for CO 2 reduction with a much more excellent CO generation rate (11.23 mol g -1 h -1 ), almost 3 times that of the pure MOFderived In 2 S 3 .
catalyst.context: In summary, In 2 S 3 NH 2 -MIL-68(In)In 2 S 3 (SMS(In)) sandwich homologous heterojunction with a hollow nanotube structure was designed and assembled by the controllable in situ sulfuration of NH 2 -MIL-68(In) for visible-light-driven CO 2 reduction.The subtle design not only maintained the original merits of NH 2 -MIL-68(In) but also endowed the composite material with dual interfacial homologous heterojunction shells, which showed enhanced adsorption and mass transfer of CO 2 , abundant reactive sites, and remarkably enhanced the separation and transfer efficiency of the photoinduced charge.As a consequence, the optimum sample (SMS(In)-7) exhibited outstanding photocatalytic activity for CO 2 reduction with a much more excellent CO generation rate (11.23 mol g -1 h -1 ), almost 3 times that of the pure MOFderived In 2 S 3 .This work proposes an instructive pathway for constructing an atomic MOF heterojunction for artificial photosynthesis. The Supporting Information is available free of charge at https:pubs.acs.orgdoi10.1021acs.iecr.0c03952. Figure 1.(a) XRD patterns and (b) FTIR spectra and (c) UV-vis DRS spectra and plots of (Ahv) 12 vs photon energy of NH 2 -MIL-68(In) and SMS(In)-X composites.TEM images of (d) SMS(In)-2, (e, j) SMS(In)-7, (f) SMS(In)-12, and (g) SMS(In)-24.(h, i) HRTEM image of the area in a yellow box.(k-n) EDX mappings of a single SMS(In) microtube. Industrial Engineering Chemistry Research mappings of a single SMS(In) hollow tube presented a uniform distribution of In, S, and O elements in the hollow nanostructure (Figure1k-n), suggesting the even interface contact between NH 2 -MIL-68(In) and In 2 S 3 .It should be noted that the distribution area of S element was slightly wider than that of the O element, further confirming the existence of In 2 S 3 on the outer surface of NH 2 -MIL-68(In). Photocatalytic Reduction of CO 2 by NH 2 -MIL-68(In) and SMS(In).The photoactivity of the samples was tested via photocatalytic CO 2 reduction reactions under irradiation.The reactions were carried out in a CH 3 CN solution with Co(bpy) 3 Figure 2. (a) CO production rate of different samples.(b) CO production rate in stability tests.(c) CO production rate under different conditions.(d) CO 2 adsorption isotherms of NH 2 -MIL-68(In), SMS(In)-7, and SMS(In)-24 (In 2 S 3 ). Figure 3. (a) PL spectra of NH 2 -MIL-68(In), SMS(In)-7, and SMS(In)-24 (pure In 2 S 3 ).(b) Time-resolved transient PL decay of SMS(In) and SMS(In)-24 (pure In 2 S 3 ).Mott-Schottky plots of (c) pure In 2 S 3 nanotubes and (d) NH 2 -MIL-68(In). FESEM and XPS spectra of NH 2 -MIL-68(In) and SMS(In); CO production rate under different light intensities; 1 H NMR spectra of liquid products; XRD patterns of SMS(In)-7 before and after reactions; CVs of Co(bpy) 3 2 ; and comparison of some typical systems (PDF)AUTHOR INFORMATION 2.1.Chemicals.Indium nitrate hydrate (In(NO 3 ) 3 xH 2 O, 99.9) was supplied by Aladdin Industrial Corporation, 2aminoterephthalic acid (NH 2 -BDC, 98.00) by TCI Shanghai Co., Ltd., and thiourea (CH 4 N 2 S, 99.0) by Damao Chemical Reagent Factory.N,N-Dimethyl-formamide (DMF, 99.5) was provided from Chinasun Specialty Products Co., Ltd. and ethanol (EtOH, 99.7) from Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd.The above-mentioned chemicals were used without further purification. light source.source: Benefiting from this unique hollow homologous heterojunction, SMS(In)-7 obtains a CO generation rate as high as 11.23 mol g -1 h -1 with visible-light irradiation, nearly 3 times enhancement over that of pure MOF-derived In 2 S 3 .
light source.context: Photocatalytic conversion of CO 2 into fuel and value-added chemicals as a promising method has been applied recently to ease energy-or environment-related crises.Herein, we construct an In 2 S 3 NH 2 -MIL-68(In) In 2 S 3 (denoted as SMS(In)) sandwich homologous heterojunction via a controllable in situ metal-organic frameworks (MOF) sulfuration process.The improved photoreduction activity mainly lies in the effective charge transfer and separation through a homogeneous interface, enhanced CO 2 adsorption and mass transfer, and plentiful reactive sites.Benefiting from this unique hollow homologous heterojunction, SMS(In)-7 obtains a CO generation rate as high as 11.23 mol g -1 h -1 with visible-light irradiation, nearly 3 times enhancement over that of pure MOF-derived In 2 S 3 .This work provides a novel insight into the utilization of MOF-based platform for an efficient atomically heterojunction design.

light source.source: In 2 S 3 NH 2 -MIL-68(In)In 2 S 3 (SMS(In)) sandwich homologous heterojunction with a hollow nanotube structure was designed and assembled by the controllable in situ sulfuration of NH 2 -MIL-68(In) for visible-light-driven CO 2 reduction.
light source.context: In summary, In 2 S 3 NH 2 -MIL-68(In)In 2 S 3 (SMS(In)) sandwich homologous heterojunction with a hollow nanotube structure was designed and assembled by the controllable in situ sulfuration of NH 2 -MIL-68(In) for visible-light-driven CO 2 reduction.The subtle design not only maintained the original merits of NH 2 -MIL-68(In) but also endowed the composite material with dual interfacial homologous heterojunction shells, which showed enhanced adsorption and mass transfer of CO 2 , abundant reactive sites, and remarkably enhanced the separation and transfer efficiency of the photoinduced charge.As a consequence, the optimum sample (SMS(In)-7) exhibited outstanding photocatalytic activity for CO 2 reduction with a much more excellent CO generation rate (11.23 mol g -1 h -1 ), almost 3 times that of the pure MOFderived In 2 S 3 .This work proposes an instructive pathway for constructing an atomic MOF heterojunction for artificial photosynthesis.
